meta.note: Named, dated forecasts from three independent houses. We show each one and the spread — we never average across houses, and we never publish our own number as data.

columns.label: Banxico survey
columns.srcName: Banco de México — Survey of Private-Sector Expectations, June 2026 (released Jul 1, 2026)

columns.label: IMF
columns.srcName: IMF World Economic Outlook, April 2026 (July update noted where it differs)

rows.note: IMF's July 2026 update trimmed 2026 to 1.2%. The Apr WEO figure (1.6%) is the last full publication; OECD is the low mark.
All three see a modest 2027 recovery on firmer US demand.
Above Banxico's 3% ± 1 target band. OECD sees inflation easing to 3.2% only by 2027; IMF does not publish a clean year-end print.
Survey sees Banxico on hold at 6.50% through year-end and into 2027.
Survey sees the peso softening modestly to 17.95 by year-end, ~18.50 in 2027.
